---
title: "Initial EDA 2"
author: "Josh"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Libraries
```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(here)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(DataExplorer)
library(skimr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

# Reading in Data & Sampling
```{r}
# Filter to trips with both pickup and dropoff in Manhattan, then take an even sample across months


files <- list.files(here("Main data"), pattern = "\\.parquet$", full.names = TRUE)

# Read in taxi zone lookup
zone_lookup <- read.csv(here("Additional Data", "NYC_Taxi_Zones.csv"))

# Get the Location IDs that belong to Manhattan
manhattan_ids <- zone_lookup %>%
  filter(Borough == "Manhattan") %>%
  pull(Location.ID)

target_n <- 10000
n_per_file <- ceiling(target_n / length(files))

sampled_data <- map_dfr(files, function(f) {
  
  df <- read_parquet(
    f,
    col_select = c(
      "tpep_pickup_datetime",
      "tpep_dropoff_datetime",
      "fare_amount",
      "trip_distance",
      "passenger_count",
      "tip_amount",
      "total_amount",
      "PULocationID",
      "DOLocationID",
      "payment_type",
      "extra"
    )
  )
  
  df %>%
    # Keep only trips where both pickup and dropoff are in Manhattan
    filter(
      PULocationID %in% manhattan_ids,
      DOLocationID %in% manhattan_ids
    ) %>%
    # Take an even sample from each file/month
    slice_sample(n = min(nrow(.), n_per_file)) %>%
    mutate(
      source_file = basename(f),
      month = str_extract(basename(f), "\\d{4}-\\d{2}")
    )
})

# Checking if there is an even sample from each of the months
sampled_data %>% count(source_file)
```

# Skim Data
```{r}
skim(sampled_data)
plot_intro(sampled_data)
plot_missing(sampled_data)

# Note: passenger_count is 22.12% missing
```

# Placeholder for Missing Values Handling
```{r}


```

# Mutate POSIX Features
```{r}
# Get useful info from the pickup and dropoff datetimes

sampled_data <- sampled_data %>%
  mutate(
    pickup_time = ymd_hms(tpep_pickup_datetime),
    dropoff_time = ymd_hms(tpep_dropoff_datetime),
    pickup_hour = hour(pickup_time),
    dropoff_hour = hour(dropoff_time),
    pickup_date = as_date(pickup_time),
    pickup_weekday_num = wday(pickup_time), # 1 = Sunday by default
    pickup_weekday_name = wday(pickup_time, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE)
  )
```

# Get Borough from LocationID
```{r}
# Left join pickup and dropoff borough into the sampled data

sampled_data <- sampled_data %>%
  left_join(
    zone_lookup %>% select(Location.ID, Borough),
    by = c("PULocationID" = "Location.ID")
  ) %>%
  rename(PUBorough = Borough) %>%
  left_join(
    zone_lookup %>% select(Location.ID, Borough),
    by = c("DOLocationID" = "Location.ID")
  ) %>%
  rename(DOBorough = Borough)

head(sampled_data$PUBorough)
head(sampled_data$DOBorough)

# If you try sampled_data$Borough, you may get an "unknown or uninitialised column"
# no more warning because Borough has been renamed to PUBorough and DOBorough.
```

# Feature Engineering -- Trip Speed
```{r}
sampled_data <- sampled_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate difference in minutes
    trip_minutes = interval(pickup_time, dropoff_time) / minutes(1),
    # Trip quickness in miles per hour
    trip_speed_mph = 60 * trip_distance / trip_minutes
  )

hist(sampled_data$trip_speed_mph)
```

# More Data Cleaning
```{r}
# Looking at ALL Yellow Trips in January

trips_01 <- trips_01 %>%
  mutate(
    pickup_time = ymd_hms(tpep_pickup_datetime),
    dropoff_time = ymd_hms(tpep_dropoff_datetime),
    # Calculate difference in minutes
    trip_minutes = interval(pickup_time, dropoff_time) / minutes(1),
    # Trip quickness in miles per hour
    trip_speed_mph = 60 * trip_distance / trip_minutes
  )

# In January - NOT looking at sampled_data but trips_01 to get better idea of pattern
sum(trips_01$trip_minutes <= 0, na.rm = TRUE) 
sum(is.na(trips_01$trip_minutes))       
sum(is.na(trips_01$pickup_time))    
sum(is.na(trips_01$dropoff_time))              

na_trips <- trips_01 %>%
  filter(is.na(trip_minutes))

# NAs in trip_minutes are due to either pickup or dropoff time being at 0:00:00
# or there being NA for dropoff time.
# Might need to remove these because they cause NA in trip_speed_mph.

sum(is.na(trips_01$trip_speed_mph))

na_trips2 <- trips_01 %>%
  filter(is.na(trip_speed_mph))

inf_trips <- trips_01 %>%
  filter(is.infinite(trip_speed_mph)) # due to trip_minutes being 0
```

# EDA for Tip %
```{r}
# Step 1: Creating Response for Tipping Behavior

# Looking at Tip %
sampled_data <- sampled_data %>%
  mutate(
    tip_pct = if_else(fare_amount > 0, tip_amount / fare_amount, NA_real_)
  )

# Capping Extreme Tip % Values between 0 and 100 whenever fare amount is too low
sampled_data <- sampled_data %>%
  mutate(
    tip_pct = if_else(fare_amount > 0, tip_amount / fare_amount, NA_real_),
    tip_pct = if_else(tip_pct < 0 | tip_pct > 1, NA_real_, tip_pct)
  )

# Step 2: Summary statistics
sampled_data %>%
  summarise(
    mean_tip = mean(tip_amount, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_tip = median(tip_amount, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_tip_pct = mean(tip_pct, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_tip_pct = median(tip_pct, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Step 3: EDA PLOTS

# Distribution of tip percentage
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = tip_pct)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Tip Percentage",
    x = "Tip Percentage",
    y = "Count"
  )

# Tip percentage by weekday
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = pickup_weekday_name, y = tip_pct)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Percentage by Pickup Weekday",
    x = "Weekday",
    y = "Tip Percentage"
  )

# Tip percentage vs trip distance
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = trip_distance, y = tip_pct)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Percentage vs Trip Distance",
    x = "Trip Distance",
    y = "Tip Percentage"
  )

# Average tip percentage by payment type
sampled_data %>%
  group_by(payment_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_tip_pct = mean(tip_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(payment_type), y = avg_tip_pct)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Average Tip Percentage by Payment Type",
    x = "Payment Type",
    y = "Average Tip Percentage"
  )

# Tip % vs fare amount

# Good way to create tip percent
sampled_data_tipP_fareAmt <- sampled_data %>%
  mutate(
    tip_pct = if_else(fare_amount > 0, 100 * tip_amount / fare_amount, NA_real_)
  )
# Plot only valid rows
tip_plot_data <- sampled_data_tipP_fareAmt %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(fare_amount),
    !is.na(tip_pct),
    is.finite(tip_pct),
    fare_amount > 0
  )

ggplot(tip_plot_data, aes(x = fare_amount, y = tip_pct)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Percentage vs Fare Amount",
    x = "Fare Amount",
    y = "Tip Percentage"
  )
```

# EDA for Tipping Amount
```{r}
# Distribution of tip amount
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = tip_amount)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Tip Amount",
    x = "Tip Amount",
    y = "Count"
  )
# Tip amount by payment type
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = factor(payment_type), y = tip_amount)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Amount by Payment Type",
    x = "Payment Type",
    y = "Tip Amount"
  )
# Tip amount by weekday
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = pickup_weekday_name, y = tip_amount)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Amount by Pickup Weekday",
    x = "Weekday",
    y = "Tip Amount"
  )
# Tip amount vs fare amount
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = fare_amount, y = tip_amount)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Amount vs Fare Amount",
    x = "Fare Amount",
    y = "Tip Amount"
  )
# Tip amount vs trip distance
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = trip_distance, y = tip_amount)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Amount vs Trip Distance",
    x = "Trip Distance",
    y = "Tip Amount"
  )
# Tip amount vs trip duration
ggplot(sampled_data, aes(x = trip_minutes, y = tip_amount)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Tip Amount vs Trip Duration",
    x = "Trip Duration (minutes)",
    y = "Tip Amount"
  )
## Comment: Two VERY LONG trips DRASTICALLY changed slope of regression line
```


# Looking at Correlation
```{r}
numeric_vars <- sampled_data %>%
  select(
    fare_amount,
    trip_distance,
    passenger_count,
    extra,
    pickup_hour,
    dropoff_hour,
    trip_minutes,
    trip_speed_mph,
    tip_amount
  )

cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_vars, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
cor_matrix

# Heatmap Version
library(corrplot)
corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper")
```

# Possible First Regression Model
```{r}
fit_tip <- lm(
  tip_amount ~ fare_amount + trip_distance + trip_minutes +
    pickup_hour + pickup_weekday_name +
    passenger_count + factor(payment_type) + extra + month,
  data = sampled_data
)

summary(fit_tip)

# Check Correlation by looking at VIF
library(car)
car::vif(fit_tip)
```
